rulings.Case Title: LISA RAYA VS. LARK CREEK INN PARTNERS L.P. AKA LARK CREED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 22, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT LARK CREEK INN PARTNERS L.P.'S, A.K.A. LARK CREEK RESTAURANT GROUP, DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED BASED SOLELY ON THE CLAIM THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT PAY THE TIPS AS REQUIRED THAT WERE GIVEN AT SPECIAL EVENTS (PARAGRAPH 28). HAVING CONSIDERED THE DEMURRER ON TWO PRIOR ALLEGATIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S PRIMARY CLAIM - THE FAILURE TO REPORT ALL TIPS TO THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION CARRIER - IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO STATE A CLAIM AS TO SUCH CONDUCT. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT EXHAUSTED HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDY: INSURANCE CODE SECTION 1871 DOES NOT CREATE A PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION. IT IS DESIGNED TO PREVENT FRAUDULENT CLAIMS TO INSURANCE CARRIERS. THE CLAIMS ALLEGED ARE SUBJECT TO THE EXCLUSIVE WORKER'S COMPENSATION REMEDY. VACANTI V STATE COMPENSATION INS. FUND (2001) 24 CAL 4TH 800, 812. P.A.G.A. IS NOT "INTENTED TO ALTER OR OTHERWISE AFFECT THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY PROVIDED BY THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION PROVISIONS OF THIS CODE." LABOR CODE SECTION 2699(K). =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: RAY BRONNER VS. RICHARD S KAIHARA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ray Bronner'S Request For The Issuance Of A Temporary Restraining Order And Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 22, 2008 LINE 15. PLAINTIFF RAY BRONNER'S REQUEST FOR THE ISSUANCE OF A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED PENDING THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT SUSTAINED THE DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. ASSUMING AN AMENDED COMPLAINT IS FILED, IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE BEEN SERVED AND THAT PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO WORKING LOCKS ON THE DOOR UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 1941.3. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN MURPHY,SR. et al VS. JOHN J. BOUVIER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defenedant John Bouvier'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 22, 2008 LINE 25. DEFENDANT JOHN BOUVIER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT IS A LICENSEE OR A TENANT GIVEN THE MARINOS' DECLARATION. HOWEVER, C.C.P. SECTION 1172 PROVIDES A DEFENSE WHERE DEFENDANT CAN SHOW LAWFUL POSSESSION FOR AT LEAST ONE YEAR AND DEFENDANT HAS DONE SO. C.C.P. 1161, AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION, APPLIES TO LICENSEE. =(302/PJM)
